headline: Taiwan overtakes India as world's 5th-largest stock market on TSMC surge
summary: Taiwan's market capitalization climbed to about $4.95T, edging past India's $4.92T to become the world's fifth-largest equity market behind the US, mainland China, Japan and Hong Kong. TSMC, up roughly 49% year-to-date and ~42% of the TAIEX, drove the benchmark to an all-time intraday high near 44,100. Foreign investors have pulled nearly $24B from Indian equities this year while rotating into Asia's AI-chip complex.

headline: AI earnings gauntlet today: Salesforce, Snowflake and Marvell report after the close
summary: A heavy slate of tracked names reports after the bell. Salesforce is seen at ~$11.06B revenue (+12.5% YoY) and $3.13 adjusted EPS, with focus on cRPO and Agentforce/Data Cloud monetization. Snowflake is expected at ~$1.32B revenue (+27% YoY). Marvell, up over 100% in 2026, is seen at ~$2.41B revenue and $0.79 EPS, with custom AI silicon (XPUs for AWS Trainium) now ~25% of datacenter revenue. Results land after today's session.

headline: GE Vernova HA gas turbine fleet tops 4 million operating hours as shares jump
summary: GE Vernova's HA-class gas turbine fleet surpassed four million cumulative commercial operating hours worldwide, underscoring reliability as datacenter-driven power demand tightens turbine slots through 2030. The milestone, alongside raised full-year guidance and a robust electrification backlog, helped lift the stock about 5.3% on May 26. The company booked $2.4B of datacenter electrification equipment orders in Q1 — more than all of last year.

headline: Memory supercycle deepens: DRAM and HBM prices seen rising another ~40% through Q2
summary: Counterpoint Research projects memory prices climbing roughly 40% through the second quarter of 2026, reinforcing views that the cycle is structural rather than a short-term bounce. SK Hynix commentary pointing to a sustained HBM supercycle has buoyed the memory complex, a core enabler of AI accelerator buildouts and a tailwind for Micron, SK Hynix and Samsung.

headline: BofA reiterates Buy on TSMC, says 'recent concerns are overdone'
summary: Bank of America reiterated its Buy rating on TSMC with a NT$2,560 price target, arguing recent concerns around the stock are overdone given durable AI-driven demand for leading-edge nodes. The call comes as TSMC's rally powers Taiwan past India in total market value.
